title: World History: Year 1 to 2026
description: Important incidents and events organized by century

events.title: Birth of Christianity
events.description: Jesus crucified in Jerusalem, founding Christianity.

events.title: Eruption of Mount Vesuvius
events.description: Destroys Pompeii and Herculaneum.

events.title: Roman Empire greatest extent under Trajan
events.description: Maximum territorial expansion.

events.title: Spread of Buddhism to China
events.description: Buddhist missionaries arrive via Silk Road.

events.title: Invention of paper in China
events.description: Cai Lun refines papermaking.

events.title: Five Good Emperors of Rome
events.description: Golden age of Roman stability and prosperity.
events.source: Gibbon, Decline and Fall; Cassius Dio

events.title: Bar Kokhba Revolt
events.description: Jewish rebellion crushed, Judea renamed Syria Palaestina.

events.title: Hadrian's Wall constructed
events.description: 73-mile defensive wall across northern Britain.

events.title: Christianity spreads throughout Empire
events.description: Christian communities in every major city by 200 AD.

events.title: Han Dynasty begins collapse
events.description: Yellow Turban Rebellion leads to Three Kingdoms.

events.title: Crisis of the Third Century
events.description: Rome nearly collapses. Over 20 emperors in 50 years.

events.title: Three Kingdoms Period in China
events.description: China split into Wei, Shu, Wu.

events.title: Goths invade Roman Empire
events.description: Emperor Decius killed at Battle of Abritus.

events.title: Diocletian becomes Emperor
events.description: Establishes Tetrarchy, last great Christian persecution.

events.title: Maya Classic Period begins
events.description: Major Maya city-states rise.
events.source: Martin & Grube, Chronicle of the Maya Kings and Queens

events.title: Constantine converts to Christianity
events.description: Vision before Battle of Milvian Bridge. First Christian Roman Emperor.

events.title: Edict of Milan
events.description: Constantine legalizes Christianity, ending centuries of persecution.

events.title: Council of Nicaea
events.description: First ecumenical council, establishes Nicene Creed.

events.title: Founding of Constantinople
events.description: Capital moved from Rome to Byzantium. Center of Eastern civilization for 1000+ years.

events.title: Battle of Adrianople
events.description: Goths defeat Romans, Emperor Valens killed. Beginning of the end for Western Rome.

events.title: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
events.description: Odoacer deposes last emperor Romulus Augustulus. Ancient world ends, Middle Ages begin.

events.title: Attila the Hun invades Europe
events.description: Devastating campaigns, 'Scourge of God.' Stopped at Catalaunian Plains.

events.title: Sack of Rome by Visigoths
events.description: First sack of Rome in 800 years. Shocks the ancient world.
events.source: Augustine, City of God; Zosimus, New History

events.title: Northern and Southern Dynasties in China
events.description: Period of division and cultural flourishing, Buddhism spreads.

events.title: Anglo-Saxon settlement of Britain
events.description: Germanic tribes migrate after Roman withdrawal, shaping English identity.
events.source: Bede, Ecclesiastical History of the English People

events.title: Justinian's Code published
events.description: Codifies Roman law into Corpus Juris Civilis, foundation of Western legal systems.

events.title: Plague of Justinian
events.description: Bubonic plague kills 25-50 million, 13-26% of world population.

events.title: Birth of Prophet Muhammad
events.description: Founder of Islam born in Mecca. Transforms Middle East and beyond.

events.title: Hagia Sophia constructed
events.description: World's largest cathedral for nearly 1000 years. Architectural marvel.

events.title: Sui Dynasty reunifies China
events.description: Reunites northern and southern China after centuries of division.

events.title: Rise of Islam and Muslim conquests
events.description: Muhammad flees Mecca to Medina (Hijra, 622). Within a century, Islamic empire stretches from Spain to India.
events.source: Ibn Ishaq, Sirat Rasul Allah; Al-Tabari, Tarikh

events.title: Tang Dynasty begins in China
events.description: China's golden age of culture, poetry, trade. Capital Chang'an becomes world's largest city.

events.title: Battle of Tours
events.description: Charles Martel defeats Umayyad Caliphate, halting Muslim expansion into Western Europe.

events.title: Viking Age begins
events.description: Raid on Lindisfarne monastery. Norse expansion reshapes Europe.
events.source: Anglo-Saxon Chronicle; Alcuin of York letters

events.title: Feudalism emerges in Europe
events.description: After Rome's fall, feudal system becomes dominant political and economic structure.

events.title: Battle of Talas
events.description: Abbasid Caliphate defeats Tang China. Papermaking transfers to Islamic world.

events.title: Charlemagne crowned Holy Roman Emperor
events.description: Unites much of Western Europe for first time since Rome.

events.title: Islamic Golden Age begins
events.description: Baghdad becomes center of science, philosophy, medicine. House of Wisdom established.

events.title: Nara Period in Japan
events.description: First permanent capital. Buddhism becomes state religion.

events.title: Borobudur temple constructed
events.description: World's largest Buddhist temple in Java.

events.title: Vikings discover Iceland
events.description: Norse explorers settle Iceland, later reaching Greenland and North America.

events.title: East-West Christian Schism begins
events.description: Growing divide between Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox churches.
events.source: Photios I, Mystagogy of the Holy Spirit

events.title: An Lushan Rebellion
events.description: Devastating Tang civil war, 13-36 million deaths. Tang never fully recovers.

events.title: Gunpowder invented in China
events.description: Chinese alchemists discover gunpowder. Transforms warfare globally.

events.title: Foundation of Kievan Rus
events.description: Oleg of Novgorod unites Eastern Slavic tribes, precursor to Russia.

events.title: Song Dynasty begins in China
events.description: Extraordinary innovation: movable type, compass, gunpowder weapons, paper money.

events.title: Leif Erikson reaches North America
events.description: First European in the Americas, 500 years before Columbus.
events.source: Saga of the Greenlanders; Saga of Erik the Red

events.title: Holy Roman Empire established
events.description: Otto I crowned Emperor. Lasts until 1806.

events.title: Great Zimbabwe constructed
events.description: Capital of Kingdom of Zimbabwe, center of gold and ivory trade.

events.title: Islamic Golden Age at peak
events.description: Alhazen (optics), Avicenna (medicine), Al-Biruni (astronomy).
events.source: Ibn al-Haytham, Book of Optics; Ibn Sina, Canon of Medicine
